Transcrição Completa: 22 Every Result Is A Marketable Headline

 

[00:00] So, every result that you get for a client is a marketable headline.

 

[00:05] You might be saying to yourself, "Well, I don't have any PR connections. You know, I don't know how to get into all these big Forbes and Inc. and Entrepreneur and Huffington. I don't have those kind of connections yet, Jeremy. I don't know how to do that. What are you talking about? Every result is a headline."

 

[00:22] Let's be clear. There's a free way for every result to be a headline.

 

[00:27] There's a really low cost. I mean, it's pretty cheap way to get a headline out there.

 

[00:32] And then yes, there's the people that you can pay off for $1,000 to $5,000 most likely to get either one or a batch of articles out there on the more credible, widely recognized, widely trafficked mediums. Okay?

 

[00:46] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "3 Tiers of PR Strategy"

 

NÚMEROS: $0 (free), $20-$50 (low cost), $1,000-$5,000 (premium)

 

VISUAL: Estrutura em três níveis: gratuito (LinkedIn/Facebook), baixo custo (Fiverr newswires), premium (Forbes/Inc/Entrepreneur), escada de autoridade crescente

 

[00:48] But the freeway is LinkedIn. It's Facebook notes typed from a business page because they allow you to literally have a headline.

 

[00:57] So let's say you have your personal brand. You go on LinkedIn. You can go to Pulse and you can publish an article.

 

[01:04] You can put a long form piece of content where literally the headline is the result that you got.

 

[01:11] Whatever your agency name is or you as if you're just operating as an individual.

 

[01:16] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Headline Examples - Results as Headlines"

 

NÚMEROS: $50,000 extra, $300,000, 8x leads

 

VISUAL: Exemplos de headlines formatadas: "Jeremy generates $50,000 extra for dentist this month", "Megalodon Marketing generates $300,000 and 8x's the leads for X business", estrutura de case study

 

[01:18] Jeremy generates $50,000 extra for dentist this month. Megalodon Marketing generates $300,000 and 8x's the leads for X business.

 

[01:29] Now, those are examples of results that get turned into a headline and then you can write a simple article.

 

[01:35] These could be three to five paragraph articles that you're putting out there.

 

[01:40] Really just case studies, but they're put out on social channels where you can actually pay to distribute them.

 

[01:47] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "LinkedIn Pulse Publishing Strategy"

 

NÚMEROS: 3-5 parágrafos, $5/dia para engagement campaigns

 

VISUAL: Fluxo de publicação: LinkedIn Pulse → artigo com foto → compartilhamento no Facebook → campanha de engajamento paga, integração multiplataforma

 

[01:49] Given that, for example, you post on LinkedIn, you automatically have a photo that you can add to that, which becomes the sharable image with it.

 

[01:57] You can publish that on a Facebook page and then now you can run an engagement campaign on that specific post targeted towards your ideal clients that you uploaded as a customer list.

 

[02:08] So you can leverage totally free channels, put $5 a day behind the engagement campaign on Facebook, just targeting people that you want to sell with your own content that you created on LinkedIn, which was also free.

 

[02:22] And really the whole thing is just premised around you creating headline driven articles from results.

 

[02:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Article Structure - Case Study Format"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente

 

VISUAL: Estrutura de artigo: headline de resultado → documentação do processo → depoimentos → serviços oferecidos, formato de case study completo

 

[02:30] And the article itself is really just the documentation of how you got the results, what the other person said.

 

[02:36] If you can capture a testimonial, of course, is fantastic to plug in there.

 

[02:40] Just the overall process that was taken. That way you can kind of throw in some of the services that you have and what other people will hit you up for.

 

[02:47] So these become great. Let's say you're just cold pitching clients. You're like, Jeremy, I'm not going to pay to distribute my articles. I'm not going to do that action.

 

[02:56] These are still beneficial to do because number one, you're likely going to forget all the awesome stuff that you do for all the clients that you're probably going to work with as an agency owner.

 

[03:06] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Benefits of Documented Timeline"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

 

VISUAL: Timeline visual de resultados com timestamps, backlog de conquistas, credibilidade construída ao longo do tempo, prova social datada

 

[03:08] Number two, people will look you up and they will see this entire backlog of results.

 

[03:13] And it's cool because it dates them. You know, it puts a time stamp on when you did what, which can make you look like an all-star. It can make your team look like allstars.

 

[03:22] Every single internal win within your company. You know, you add another staff member, you add another key player into the mix, your key player did something in their personal life that was awesome.

 

[03:32] Everything becomes a marketable headline that acts as a timeline of the results that you've gotten.

 

[03:38] That way when people look you up in the future, you look like the shit man or the woman.

 

[03:43] All because you have a documented timeline of your results.

 

[03:47] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Level Up: Fiverr.com Newswire Strategy"

 

NÚMEROS: $20-$50 por serviço, 400-500 distribuidores, 2-3 parágrafos

 

VISUAL: Plataforma Fiverr destacada, busca por ABC/NBC/CBS/Fox, estrutura de newswire, rede de distribuição de mídia local

 

[03:49] Now, the level up from the free version, okay, you can go on fiverr.com. It's a great practical piece of advice here for you to use.

 

[03:58] Fiverr.com. Go in and just type into the search bar NBC, ABC, Fox, and there's another one. I can't remember what it is, but CBS, there it is.

 

[04:09] So, ABC, NBC, CBS, and Fox. You can just type in any one of those.

 

[04:14] You're going to see about 50 different people that are offering you the opportunity to have a two to three paragraph PR piece that gets published on what's called a newswire that gets picked up by about 4 to 500 different distributors that all are local channels that are NBC, ABC, CBS or Fox.

 

[04:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Newswire Distribution Results"

 

NÚMEROS: 400-500 distribuidores, $20-$50 custo, canais locais de 4 grandes redes

 

VISUAL: Mapa de distribuição de mídia, logos de ABC/NBC/CBS/Fox, alcance de canais locais, exemplo de Miami e Beverly Hills mencionados

 

[04:37] A few of them will be bigger. For example, I put out a few different articles through those newswires using fiverr.com.

 

[04:44] This is about a $20 to a $50 service that you're going to pay.

 

[04:49] And the more expensive it is means the longer the actual piece is going to be.

 

[04:53] So you can spend 20 bucks to get two paragraphs written about you in a headline and then have those published on all these local news channels and then it makes it gives you the ability to put that on your website that you were featured on ABC, NBC, CBS and Fox and it cost you 20 to 50 bucks.

 

[05:12] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Authority Hijacking Applications"

 

NÚMEROS: $20-$50 investimento

 

VISUAL: Logos de mídia no website, footer de email com credenciais, badges "As Featured On", authority positioning visual

 

[05:14] You can also put that in your email footer. You also get some great search traction on some of these that are indexed. Not all of them are but the majority of them will be.

 

[05:24] You can use these things to run engagement campaigns with on Facebook targeted towards the people that you want to sell or just people that you want to keep yourself in front of so you can continue building your authority and positioning yourself and using a little authority hijacking at the same time.

 

[05:39] There's a very practical way for you to gain, you know, 20 to 50 bucks some authority and some expert positioning and publish the results that you're getting.

 

[05:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Newswire Quality Indicators"

 

NÚMEROS: 400-500 links (bom), 100-200 links (robótico)

 

VISUAL: Comparação de qualidade: distribuição automática vs. seleção manual por jornalistas, indicadores de performance de newswire

 

[05:52] To be clear, you can really publish anything. The more people that pick it up means the more likely it is to be read by those people's channels and platforms that they're distributing on.

 

[06:03] If you're not getting between four or 500 links per PR piece that you're doing, maybe you're getting 100 to 200, that means it was likely robotic or an automatic process for that newswire that got put out just to be automatically picked up and published on those sites.

 

[06:20] When you're in the four to 500 range and you pay that $20 to $50 fee of how many people will distribute your specific marketed result, that's great.

 

[06:30] That means that not only did you have the automation come and pick your article up off the newswire, it means that you had real people who were journalists sit there and say, "You know what? This is something I want to publish on my channel here and wherever."

 

[06:43] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Major Market Success Examples"

 

NÚMEROS: 4-8 milhões de pessoas (Miami/Beverly Hills)

 

VISUAL: Exemplos de publicações: Los Angeles Times, Channel 4 News, grandes mercados metropolitanos, alcance orgânico em cidades principais

 

[06:45] When I was operating out of Miami and Beverly Hills at the same time, I would frequently when I was publishing my results like this get picked up in Los Angeles Times, Channel 4 News, which is big out there.

 

[06:57] And these are big cities, you know, four to eight million people in them given the time of year and the amount of tourists there.

 

[07:03] So all that becomes just additional organic eyeballs that you can have see you, your company, and what you do.

 

[07:10] But to be clear, I like it more than anything else just for the timeline of documented results.

 

[07:15] So when people look you up, you have things in the search results. You have things that you can actually run small engagement campaign budgets on just to keep yourself looking awesome.

 

[07:26] But in addition to that, it helps inspire your team. Okay.

 

[07:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Tier 3: Premium PR Placements"

 

NÚMEROS: $1,000-$5,000 investimento, 6 meses a 1 ano em negócio recomendado

 

VISUAL: Logos premium: Forbes, Inc., Entrepreneur, Huffington Post, escada de qualificação, requisitos de publicação

 

[07:32] Now, the most the third tier to this, which is kind of the most expensive, between $1,000 and $5,000 bucks.

 

[07:39] Like I said, given the person that you're doing this with, you can get press in these different distribution channels like Forbes, Inc., Entrepreneur, Huffington Post, and there's kind of a game of stepping stones in PR.

 

[07:53] So, I don't want to give you the impression that if you're an absolute nobody right now and you've never gotten any results for somebody that you can just pay somebody 5K and get in Forbes right away.

 

[08:03] They do have standards and they do qualify who they take onto their platforms.

 

[08:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Premium PR Qualification Requirements"

 

NÚMEROS: 6 meses a 1 ano mínimo em negócio

 

VISUAL: Critérios de qualificação: tempo em negócio, tamanho de impacto (médio a grande), alcance não-local, resultados significativos, standards editoriais

 

[08:10] I'm not telling you this so it demotivates you or puts you in a position where you don't leverage it. I just want to be direct with you and let you know you do need to be somewhat positioned maybe about six months to a year in business at least and doing something that is of medium to big action size.

 

[08:27] You know, not small actions, not local actions. You know, you're really making an impact either in somebody's business or solving a particular problem that a lot of people can identify with if you're going to get into those channels.

 

[08:40] Otherwise, even paying it off, you might actually have that money refunded to you because the person who you're doing it through might find that it's hard to publish whatever you want published.

 

[08:51] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Vetting Premium PR Providers"

 

NÚMEROS: 1-3 meses de dry spell (evitar), máximo 1x por mês (evitar)

 

VISUAL: Red flags: períodos sem publicações, baixa frequência de posts, sinais de alerta ao contratar, critérios de qualificação de fornecedores

 

[08:53] If you're going to pay between that $1,000, $5,000 range, make sure you're paying people who are actually consistent with doing this for other people.

 

[09:01] Don't hire the people. I've had this happen a few times. Don't hire the people that have dry spells where they can't publish anything for like one to three months.

 

[09:10] You know, don't publish with people or pay people who can't even post more than once a month.

 

[09:15] You know, if they're a contributor themselves, they have a limit for how much they can put off on these different platforms.

 

[09:21] If you're paying somebody who's going to pay publishers on those platforms, that middleman once again just needs to be somebody qualified who's consistently getting this done for other clients as well.

 

[09:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "PR Pricing Variables"

 

NÚMEROS: $1,000-$5,000 range dependendo do canal

 

VISUAL: Variáveis de custo: canal de publicação, tipo de fornecedor (contributor vs. middleman), frequência de publicação, histórico de resultados

 

[09:34] You'll typically find depending on the channel, of course, dictates the cost when you're paying people off.

 

[09:40] And to be very clear, there's a ton of people, more without exaggerating, there's about 10 to 15 different press related agencies that are very credible inside of our own community that only do press, that only distribute PR to all those big channels.

 

[09:57] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Strategic Referral Network - PR Agencies"

 

NÚMEROS: 10-15 agências credíveis na comunidade

 

VISUAL: Rede de parceiros especializados em PR, oportunidades de referência para clientes, especialização em distribuição para grandes canais

 

[10:00] Somebody that you could have as your strategic referral network when you have clients who need press.

 

[10:05] So, all in all, hope this helps you.

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

Tier 1 (Gratuito): $0 - LinkedIn Pulse + Facebook Notes

Tier 2 (Baixo Custo): $20-$50 - Fiverr newswires

Tier 3 (Premium): $1,000-$5,000 - Forbes/Inc/Entrepreneur/Huffington Post

Distribuição Newswire: 400-500 links (qualidade), 100-200 links (robótico)

Engagement Campaigns: $5/dia no Facebook

Alcance de Mercado: 4-8 milhões de pessoas (Miami/Beverly Hills)

Qualificação Premium: 6 meses a 1 ano mínimo em negócio

PR Agencies na Comunidade: 10-15 agências credíveis

Exemplos de Headlines: $50,000 extra, $300,000 gerados, 8x leads

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Headline-Driven Articles: Transformar cada resultado em headline marketável

LinkedIn Pulse Publishing: Artigos de 3-5 parágrafos com case studies

Cross-Platform Distribution: LinkedIn → Facebook → Engagement Campaigns

Fiverr Newswire Strategy: Buscar ABC/NBC/CBS/Fox para distribuição em massa

Authority Hijacking: Usar logos de mídia em website e email footer

Timeline Documentation: Criar backlog datado de todos os resultados

SEO Indexing: Maioria dos newswires são indexados para busca orgânica

Team Inspiration: Publicar vitórias internas e conquistas da equipe

Strategic Referral Network: Parceria com agências especializadas em PR

Quality Indicators: Verificar 400-500 links e seleção manual por jornalistas

 

ESTRUTURA:

3 Tiers de PR:

Tier 1: Gratuito (LinkedIn/Facebook)

Tier 2: Baixo custo (Fiverr newswires)

Tier 3: Premium (publicações de alto nível)

Formato de Artigo: Headline → Processo → Depoimentos → Serviços

Stepping Stones em PR: Progressão gradual de autoridade

Qualificação para Premium: Tempo + Impacto + Alcance não-local

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

ROI Tier 1: $0 investimento + $5/dia em ads = autoridade gratuita

ROI Tier 2: $20-$50 = 400-500 publicações em canais locais de grandes redes

ROI Tier 3: $1,000-$5,000 = publicação em Forbes/Inc/Entrepreneur

Custo por Parágrafo: Mais caro = artigo mais longo (Fiverr)

Risco de Reembolso: Possível se não qualificado para premium

Aplicações: Website badges, email footer, engagement campaigns, SEO

Valor de Autoridade: Timeline documentada = credibilidade composta

 

DURAÇÃO: ~10 minutos FORMATO: Vídeo educacional sobre estratégias de PR e marketing de resultados em três níveis de investimento
